perturbation.original_text: 1.70 "Non-SHP2 Collaboration Product" means for any Drug Treatment Regimen under the Collaboration that is [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.70 "Non-SHP2 Collaboration Product" means for any Drug Treatment Regimen under the Collaboration that is a combination therapy including a SHP2 Inhibitor.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction because the original text defines a Non-SHP2 Collaboration Product as something other than a SHP2 inhibitor, while the modified text defines it as a combination therapy including a SHP2 Inhibitor, directly contradicting each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Non-SHP2 Collaboration Product" means for any Drug Treatment Regimen under the Collaboration that is a combination therapy including a SHP2 Inhibitor.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Non-SHP2 Collaboration Product" as something *other* than a SHP2 inhibitor. The changed text explicitly defines it as *including* a SHP2 Inhibitor. This creates a direct in-text contradiction as it redefines a key term in a directly opposite way within the same document, meeting the criteria for Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: 1.17 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means: (a) with respect to Sanofi, [***], consistent with [***] that [***], taking into account [***], including [***] and (b) with respect to RevMed, [***], consistent with [***] that [***], taking into account [***], including [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.17 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means: (a) with respect to Sanofi, using all available resources and personnel to maximize success, and (b) with respect to RevMed, making a good faith attempt but not necessarily the most strenuous effort to achieve the goals. consistent with [***] that [***], taking into account [***], including [***].
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity to the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts'. The change made in Sanofi is more demanding that RevMed. This will introduce confusion within the text as for the standard of effort the parties shall have to take on the collaboration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consistent with [***] or as otherwise agreed by the Parties, Sanofi shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts [***] to file and seek approval for an MAA for at least one Product in all of such countries or, in the case of the Major Market Countries in the European Union, through the centralized European Union approval process. If Sanofi materially breaches its obligation set forth in this Section 5.7, [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces different standards of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' for Sanofi and RevMed, which contradicts the requirement that Sanofi uses 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to file and seek approval. The ambiguity around the standard of effort required directly impacts the enforceability of Sanofi's obligations under Section 5.7, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition. The phrase 'using all available resources and personnel to maximize success' for Sanofi is clearly different from 'making a good faith attempt but not necessarily the most strenuous effort' for RevMed. If both parties are require to do 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' then they should both have equivalent definitions.

perturbation.original_text: 9.5 Payment Terms; Exchange Rate. Notwithstanding any term to the contrary of this Agreement, RevMed shall deliver an invoice to Sanofi for all payments owed by Sanofi to RevMed under this Agreement. Sanofi will make all payments owed to RevMed within [***] after the date on which Sanofi receives an undisputed invoice for such owed amount, except where a different timeframe is expressly provided in another Section of this Agreement (e.g., for the reimbursement of RevMed R&D Costs pursuant to Sections 4.5 and 5.5; the payment of the buy-in payment pursuant to Section 5.6(b)B; the upfront payment set forth in Section 9.1; the royalties payable pursuant to Section 9.3, the payment of VAT pursuant to Section 9.7(b); and the payment of unpaid or overpaid amounts pursuant to Section 9.9(b)).
perturbation.changed_text: 9.5 Payment Terms; Exchange Rate. Notwithstanding any term to the contrary of this Agreement, RevMed shall deliver an invoice to Sanofi for all payments owed by Sanofi to RevMed under this Agreement. Sanofi will make all payments owed to RevMed within [***] after the date on which Sanofi receives an undisputed invoice for such owed amount, except where a different timeframe is expressly provided in another Section of this Agreement (e.g., for the upfront payment set forth in Section 9.1).
perturbation.explanation: By removing some of the exceptions, there is ambiguity as to what timeframe will govern the payments. The text makes it unclear whether those exceptions of the timeframe should be applied, or not. This can lead to multiple interpretations and disputes as a result.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Calendar Years 2018, 2021 and All Calendar Years After 2021. Sanofi shall be responsible for 100% of the Research and Development Costs for Calendar Years 2018, 2021 and all Calendar Years after 2021. Sanofi will reimburse RevMed for any RevMed R&D Costs incurred by or on behalf of RevMed after the Execution Date in the performance of its activities under the Research Plan, provided that such RevMed R&D Costs are incurred per the Research Budget for such activities as approved by the JSC and [***] set forth in the Research Budget for the particular Calendar Quarter. Promptly following the end of each Calendar Quarter during which RevMed is responsible for activities under the Research Plan, but in no event later than [***] following the end of such Calendar Quarter, RevMed will provide to Sanofi a detailed expense report in form approved by the JRDC with respect to the RevMed R&D Costs incurred by or on behalf of RevMed during such Calendar Quarter consistent with the previous sentence (including, if requested by Sanofi in writing, copies of receipts or invoices from Third Parties for all RevMed R&D Out-of-Pocket Costs) together with an invoice for the same, provided that[***]. Sanofi will reimburse RevMed in Dollars all undisputed amounts within such expense reports under this Section 4.5 within [***] following receipt of the invoice therefor. RevMed shall invoice Sanofi for costs under this Section 4.5 on an accrual basis.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 9.5 lists specific sections (4.5 and 5.5, 5.6(b)B, 9.1, 9.3, 9.7(b), and 9.9(b)) that are exceptions to the standard payment timeframe. The changed text removes several of these exceptions, including the one relating to Section 4.5 (R&D costs). Section 4.5 specifies a payment timeframe for R&D costs ("\[***] following receipt of the invoice therefor"). By removing the reference to Section 4.5 as an exception in Section 9.5, it creates a direct contradiction. It is no longer clear whether the standard payment timeframe in Section 9.5 or the specific timeframe in Section 4.5 applies to the reimbursement of RevMed R&D Costs, leading to a strong in-text contradiction and ambiguities as defined in the prompt.
